name: Apollo.io
description: An all-in-one sales intelligence and engagement platform that combines a massive B2B contact database (275M+ contacts) with email sequencing, calling, and analytics. Apollo is popular with startups and growth-stage companies because it replaces multiple tools at a fraction of the cost.
whyUseIt: Apollo solves two problems at once: finding prospects (with its built-in database) and reaching them (with its sequence and calling tools). For teams that can't afford separate tools for data, engagement, and analytics, Apollo provides 80% of the functionality at 20% of the price. It's particularly strong for building targeted prospect lists quickly.
aiCapabilities: Apollo AI scores leads, suggests personalised email copy, recommends optimal send times, and provides intent signals showing which accounts are actively researching your product category.
funnelPosition: Top of funnel — lead sourcing, prospecting, and initial outreach
sellerWorkflow: SDRs search the database for prospects matching the ICP, build lists, enrich contact data, and enrol prospects into sequences — all within one tool. AEs use Apollo for account research and signal monitoring.
managerExpects: Prospect lists aligned to ICP, consistent sequence execution, engagement metrics reviewed weekly, and CRM sync working cleanly so nothing falls through the cracks.
demoDescription: Search the contact database with ICP filters, build a prospect list, enrich contacts with emails and phone numbers, create a multi-step sequence, and view engagement analytics.

vocabulary.definition: Behavioural data showing a company is actively researching topics related to your product — a buying signal.
The process of adding missing data (email, phone, title, company info) to a contact record.

beginnerMistakes: Sending to unverified emails — always use Apollo's email verification before sequencing
Building lists that are too broad instead of tightly matching your ICP
Not syncing Apollo with your CRM — creating duplicate contacts and lost data
